title: phaca diurna
intro: Phaca diurna, also known as the wild indigo or desert indigo, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Phaca diurna include its use in treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce pain. In herbal preparations, Phaca diurna is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
